name: Subset Relation is Reflexive
guide_hints: # Level 2: First we will define the subset relation then prove that it is reflexive.
In ZFC, every object is basically set(literally every). By object I mean any term(which also includes constants and variables)
We say a set A is subset of B if every element of A is also an element of B. You can type *option + 2286* to type ⊆ in Macbook.
Type `cf subset "?t1 ⊆ ?t2" ∀?v3 ?v3 ∈ ?t1 ⇒ ?v3 ∈ ?t2` to define the binary subset relation. I hope you can see how the logic is working.
Type `cv A`, then `cf goal A⊆A` then `mission goal` as it is the formula which we want to prove.
Type `rw subset` which should ask you for a new variable to enter. You can type `x` to change the goal formula to `∀x x∈A ⇒ x∈A`
Type `intro y` to change the goal formula to `y∈A ⇒ y∈A`.
Type `apply PC1` to close the mission goal.
